mechanism_narrative: An increase in the effective federal funds rate from the prior month signals tighter liquidity conditions that raise discount rates and reduce equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of months when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the t-statistic of return differences is insignificant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate indicate a stagflationary impulse where rising inflation forces additional monetary tightening, jointly compressing real earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 40% of joint-increase observations, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (p-value > 0.05) in a logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing contraction regime, a month-over-month decline in housing starts signals deteriorating construction activity and weakening mortgage-backed credit markets, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from peak in 2006 to 1467 in Jun 2007
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of observations when HOUST declines, or the signal's information coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) during identified housing contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals negative supply shocks and reduced corporate revenue capacity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns follow INDPRO declines in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates immediate labor market weakening that reduces consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in > 55% of months when PAYEMS declines, or the t-statistic of the difference in means between decline and non-decline months is not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales signals resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in > 45% of months when RSAFS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of quarters when GDPC1 grows, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
